title: Foldable Wood Bath Tray
summary: A two-board tray that hinges flat for storage and spans the tub when open — build it to the exact width of your own bathtub.
description:
This is a build-to-fit project: the finished length is whatever your tub's rail-to-rail span is, so measure your own tub before cutting anything. The plan here uses a 36" example, which is a common width, but the method scales to any length.

Two 1x6 boards joined by a simple hinge is the whole mechanism — one side gets a 1x2 lip glued along its outer edge to keep a book, phone, or wine glass from sliding off, and the hinge lets the whole tray fold flat for storage between uses instead of taking up closet space fully open.

materials.name: Pine, 1x6, 8 ft
materials.note: Yields both boards for a 36" tray — buy a longer board for a wider tub.

materials.name: Wood poly or clear coat
materials.note: A water-resistant finish matters more here than on almost any other build — this piece sits over a wet tub.

cutList.note: Build to your own tub's width — 36" is an example.

steps.title: Measure your tub
steps.body: Measure the distance between the inside edges of your tub's rails where the tray will rest. That number is your finished tray length — cut both 1x6 boards and the 1x2 lip to match it, not to the 36" example in the cut list.

steps.title: Attach the edge lip
steps.body: Nail and glue the 1x2 lip along one long edge of one of the 1x6 boards. This becomes the tray's front edge, keeping items from sliding off.

steps.title: Hinge the two halves together
steps.body: Place the second 1x6 board next to the first and attach them with the hinge along the facing edges, so the two boards fold together like a book. Check that it folds flat and opens back out to span your tub cleanly before finishing.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand everything smooth, then stain and seal with a water-resistant poly or clear coat — this piece lives in a wet environment, so don't skip the finish.

images.alt: A wooden bath tray spanning a bathtub, folded from two hinged boards
